stories.headline: China's Kimi K3 just became the cheapest frontier AI model
stories.blurb: Moonshot AI's new Kimi K3 model matches GPT-4o and Claude 3.5 Sonnet on key benchmarks while costing a fraction of the price—roughly $0.14 per million input tokens versus $2.50-$3 for Western alternatives. The model also handles 128K context windows and can process images, making it a serious budget option for developers willing to work with a Chinese provider.
stories.why_it_matters: Frontier performance at one-tenth the API cost changes the economics of AI deployment
stories.original_headline: China has a new top model

stories.headline: Claude can now pull your passwords from 1Password
stories.blurb: 1Password's new browser integration lets you authorize Claude to grab your login credentials and use them to complete tasks like booking flights or managing accounts. You approve access per-task instead of manually copying passwords into the chatbot.
stories.why_it_matters: AI agents just got closer to actually handling your errands instead of just drafting the emails.
stories.original_headline: Claude can now use your 1Password credentials for you

stories.headline: Google Vids now lets you generate and star in videos with AI avatars
stories.blurb: Google Vids added two features: Gemini Omni generates full videos from text prompts (script, visuals, voiceover), and Personal Avatars let you create a digital version of yourself that speaks your script without recording each time. Both ship to Workspace users starting this week.
stories.why_it_matters: You can now ship explainer videos or training content without filming or hiring talent.
stories.original_headline: Create, edit and star in videos with two Google Vids updates

stories.headline: Roblox now lets you build a game from a single text prompt
stories.blurb: Roblox just added a 'Build' feature to its mobile app that generates playable games from a text description. Type what you want, and the AI creates a basic game you can jump into and customize.
stories.why_it_matters: Game creation just went from scripting to typing—no code, no desktop required.
stories.original_headline: Roblox launches an AI-powered game-creation feature in its mobile app

try_this_prompt.title: The skill gap translator
try_this_prompt.prompt: I want to learn something new and I'll describe it below. Don't give me a course list. Instead: tell me the three sub-skills that actually matter, which one I should ignore completely, and the smallest project that would force me to learn all three at once.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: When you're tired of bookmarking tutorials you'll never finish.

daily_question: If AI tools keep getting cheaper and more accessible, what skill are you doubling down on to stay valuable?
